message:
Fix color detection to check stderr instead of stdout

All log functions output to stderr (>&2), but color detection was
checking if stdout (-t 1) is a terminal. This caused incorrect behavior:
- Colors disabled when stdout redirected but stderr goes to terminal
- Colors enabled when stderr goes to file but stdout is terminal

Changed -t 1 to -t 2 in both ru and install.sh to properly detect if
stderr (where colored output goes) is connected to a terminal.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-104","title":"Create .github/workflows directory","description":"**Create GitHub workflows directory structure**

## What
Create .github/workflows/ directory with placeholder ci.yml and release.yml files.

## Why
Establishes CI/CD structure. Even placeholder files communicate intent and can be fleshed out incrementally.

## Implementation
```bash
mkdir -p .github/workflows
touch .github/workflows/ci.yml
touch .github/workflows/release.yml
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- .github/workflows/ directory exists
- ci.yml and release.yml files exist (can be minimal placeholders)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:20.264550091-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:20.264550091-05:00","close_reason":"Phase 1 remaining tasks completed","labels":["ci","setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-104","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.832533978-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-105","title":"Create examples/ directory with sample lists","description":"**Create examples directory with sample repo lists**

## What
Create examples/ directory containing public.txt (with example repos) and private.template.txt (empty template).

## Why
Users need examples to understand the list format. The template shows structure without committing actual private repos.

## Content: examples/public.txt
```
# Example public repositories
# Add your own repos below

# Format examples:
# https://github.com/owner/repo
# owner/repo
# owner/repo@branch
# owner/repo as local-name

charmbracelet/gum
cli/cli
```

## Content: examples/private.template.txt
```
# Private repositories template
# Copy this to ~/.config/ru/repos.d/private.txt
# Add your private repos below

```

## Acceptance Criteria
- examples/public.txt exists with example content
- examples/private.template.txt exists as empty template
- Format examples are documented in comments","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:20.267036655-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:20.267036655-05:00","close_reason":"Phase 1 remaining tasks completed","labels":["docs","setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-105","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.860720146-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-106","title":"Ensure no je_*.txt files in repo","description":"**Remove/ignore legacy je_*.txt files**

## What
Ensure no je_public_github_repos.txt or similar development artifacts are committed.

## Why
These were development files from the original sync script. They shouldn't ship - user lists belong in XDG config, not the repo. Their presence confuses the packaging story.

## Implementation
- Add je_*.txt to .gitignore
- If any exist, remove from git tracking: `git rm --cached je_*.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- No je_*.txt files in repository
- Pattern added to .gitignore for safety","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:20.26860041-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:16:20.26860041-05:00","close_reason":"Phase 1 remaining tasks completed","labels":["cleanup","setup"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-106","depends_on_id":"bd-1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.889644175-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-11","title":"Phase 11: Reporting & Summary","description":"**EPIC: Summary Reports & Conflict Resolution Help**

## Goal
Generate beautiful summary reports and actionable conflict resolution guidance.

## Rationale
After processing dozens of repos, users need a clear summary: what succeeded, what failed, what needs attention. For repos with issues, we provide copy-pasteable commands to resolve them.

## Summary Components
- Counts: cloned, updated, current, conflicts, failed
- Timing: total duration, per-phase breakdowns
- Log location: where to find detailed logs

## Conflict Resolution Help
For each problematic repo, we show:
- What the issue is (dirty tree, diverged, auth failure)
- Multiple resolution options with exact commands
- Risk level of each option (e.g., 'DESTRUCTIVE' warning)

## JSON Report
In --json mode, stdout gets a structured report with all details, suitable for further processing or dashboards.

## Success Criteria
- Summary displays after every sync/status run
- Conflict resolution commands are correct and copy-pasteable
- JSON output is valid and complete","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T17:29:44.751535567-05:00","labels":["reporting","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11","depends_on_id":"bd-4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.424659852-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-11","depends_on_id":"bd-10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.452858654-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1101","title":"Implement aggregate_results()","description":"**Parse NDJSON results file**

## What
Function to aggregate results from the temp NDJSON file into summary counts.

## Why
After processing, we need to summarize: how many cloned, updated, failed, etc.

## Implementation
```bash
aggregate_results() {
    local cloned=0 updated=0 current=0 failed=0 conflicts=0 skipped=0
    
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        local status
        status=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.status')
        
        case "$status" in
            ok)       ((cloned++)) ;;
            updated)  ((updated++)) ;;
            current)  ((current++)) ;;
            failed)   ((failed++)) ;;
            diverged|conflict) ((conflicts++)) ;;
            *)        ((skipped++)) ;;
        esac
    done < "$RESULTS_FILE"
    
    echo "CLONED=$cloned UPDATED=$updated CURRENT=$current FAILED=$failed CONFLICTS=$conflicts SKIPPED=$skipped"
}
```

## Note on jq
jq is optional but makes parsing easier. Fall back to grep/sed if unavailable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Counts all status types
- Handles empty file
- Works without jq (fallback)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["reporting"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1101","depends_on_id":"bd-11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.241751016-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1101","depends_on_id":"bd-405","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.271896596-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1102","title":"Implement print_summary()","description":"**Print styled summary box**

## What
Function to display the final summary in a beautiful box.

## Why
A clear summary shows users what happened at a glance.

## Output Design
```
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│                    Sync Summary                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Cloned:     8 repos                                        │
│  Updated:   31 repos                                        │
│  Current:    3 repos (already up to date)                   │
│  Conflicts:  2 repos (need attention)                       │
│  Failed:     0 repos                                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Total: 47 repos processed in 2m 34s                        │
│  Logs: ~/.local/state/ru/logs/2026-01-03/                   │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Implementation
Use gum style for box if available, ANSI fallback otherwise.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shows all counts
- Shows timing
- Shows log location
- Beautiful with gum, acceptable without","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["reporting","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1102","depends_on_id":"bd-1101","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.301743123-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-1102","depends_on_id":"bd-504","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.332586268-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1103","title":"Implement print_conflict_help()","description":"**Print actionable conflict resolution**

## What
For repos with issues, show exactly how to resolve them.

## Why
Users shouldn't have to figure out resolution commands themselves.

## Output Design
```
Repositories Needing Attention
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

1. mcp_agent_mail
   Path:   /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail
   Branch: main
   Issue:  Dirty working tree (3 files modified)

   Resolution options:
     a) Stash and pull:
        cd /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail && git stash && git pull && git stash pop

     b) Commit your changes:
        cd /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail && git add . && git commit -m "WIP"

     c) Discard local changes (DESTRUCTIVE):
        cd /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail && git checkout . && git clean -fd
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Lists all problematic repos
- Shows specific issue
- Provides copy-paste commands
- Warns about destructive options","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["reporting"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1103","depends_on_id":"bd-1101","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:12.362760602-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -49,10 +49,11 @@ GITHUB_API="https://api.github.com"
 GITHUB_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com"
 
 #==============================================================================
-# COLORS (disabled if not a terminal or NO_COLOR is set)
+# COLORS (disabled if stderr is not a terminal or NO_COLOR is set)
+# We check -t 2 (stderr) because all log functions output to stderr
 #==============================================================================
 
-if [[ -t 1 ]] && [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]]; then
+if [[ -t 2 ]] && [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]]; then
     RED='\033[0;31m'
     GREEN='\033[0;32m'
     YELLOW='\033[0;33m'

@@ -96,8 +96,9 @@ DEFAULT_PARALLEL="1"
 # SECTION 2: ANSI COLOR DEFINITIONS
 #==============================================================================
 
-# Colors (disabled if not a terminal or NO_COLOR is set)
-if [[ -t 1 ]] && [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]]; then
+# Colors (disabled if stderr is not a terminal or NO_COLOR is set)
+# We check -t 2 (stderr) because all log functions output to stderr
+if [[ -t 2 ]] && [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" ]]; then
     RED='\033[0;31m'
     GREEN='\033[0;32m'
     YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
@@ -188,11 +189,12 @@ write_result() {
     local message="${5:-}"
 
     if [[ -n "$RESULTS_FILE" ]]; then
-        # Escape message for JSON safety
-        local safe_message
+        # Escape all string fields for JSON safety
+        local safe_repo safe_message
+        safe_repo=$(json_escape "$repo_name")
         safe_message=$(json_escape "$message")
         printf '{"repo":"%s","action":"%s","status":"%s","duration":%s,"message":"%s","timestamp":"%s"}\n' \
-            "$repo_name" "$action" "$status" "${duration:-0}" "$safe_message" "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
+            "$safe_repo" "$action" "$status" "${duration:-0}" "$safe_message" "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
             >> "$RESULTS_FILE"
     fi
 }
